feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: Assistant Professor (Selection Grade) at UPES Dehradun, researcher in Blockchain, IoT, Edge AI, Federated Learning, and Multimodal Machine Learning.

entry.title: Sunrise at the Coast
entry.content: The sea, the light, and the early silence made this stop unforgettable. This travel note is a placeholder for your future journey stories.
entry.summary: A calm travel note from the shoreline at first light.

entry.title: Assistant Professor (Selection Grade) at UPES Dehradun

link.title: Assistant Professor (Selection Grade) at UPES Dehradun

entry.content: I am serving as Assistant Professor (Selection Grade) at UPES Dehradun. My current work focuses on Blockchain, IoT, Edge AI, Federated Learning, and Multimodal Machine Learning for intelligent systems and next-generation transportation research.
entry.summary: Career update from my academic profile

entry.title: Slow Mornings in the Hills
entry.content: This is a starter travel post for your blog card layout. You can later replace it with your own destination story, photos, and memories.
entry.summary: Reflections from a quiet mountain stay.

entry.title: Completed FDP from IIT Guwahati
entry.content:
Completed a one-week Faculty Development Programme (FDP) organized by the E&ICT Academy, IIT Guwahati in association with UPES Dehradun.

Programme Details

  • Title: Advanced Architectures and Real-Time Systems for Intelligent Embedded Applications
  • Duration: January 05-09, 2026
  • Organizer: E&ICT Academy, Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati
  • Associated Institute: University of Petroleum and Energy Studies (UPES), Dehradun
  • Reference No.: EICTP-ITOF/25-26/009010

Highlights

This FDP focused on advanced computing architectures and real-time systems for intelligent embedded applications. The programme aligned well with my academic interests in intelligent systems, embedded computing, Blockchain, IoT, Edge AI, and next-generation research-driven teaching.

It also contributed to my continued professional development as Assistant Professor (Selection Grade) at UPES Dehradun, strengthening my engagement with emerging technologies and applied interdisciplinary research.

entry.title: Notebook from the Road
entry.content: This entry gives your new travel blog section a third card, matching the project-style layout you wanted for the Blog page.
entry.summary: Small observations, long roads, and meaningful pauses.

entry.title: a post with plotly.js
entry.content:
This is an example post with some plotly code.

```plotly
{
  "data": [
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [10, 15, 13, 17],
      "type": "scatter"
    },
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [16, 5, 11, 9],
      "type": "scatter"
    }
  ]
}
```

Which generates:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [10, 15, 13, 17],
      "type": "scatter"
    },
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [16, 5, 11, 9],
      "type": "scatter"
    }
  ]
}

Also another example chart.

```plotly
{
  "data": [
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [10, 15, 13, 17],
      "mode": "markers"
    },
    {
      "x": [2, 3, 4, 5],
      "y": [16, 5, 11, 9],
      "mode": "lines"
    },
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [12, 9, 15, 12],
      "mode": "lines+markers"
    }
  ],
  "layout": {
    "title": {
      "text": "Line and Scatter Plot"
    }
  }
}
```

This is how it looks like:

{
  "data": [
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [10, 15, 13, 17],
      "mode": "markers"
    },
    {
      "x": [2, 3, 4, 5],
      "y": [16, 5, 11, 9],
      "mode": "lines"
    },
    {
      "x": [1, 2, 3, 4],
      "y": [12, 9, 15, 12],
      "mode": "lines+markers"
    }
  ],
  "layout": {
    "title": {
      "text": "Line and Scatter Plot"
    }
  }
}
entry.summary: this is what included plotly.js code could look like

entry.title: a post with image galleries
entry.content:
The images in this post are all zoomable, arranged into different mini-galleries using different libraries.

Lightbox2

PhotoSwipe

Spotlight JS

Venobox
entry.summary: this is what included image galleries could look like

entry.title: a post with tabs
entry.content:
This is how a post with tabs looks like. Note that the tabs could be used for different purposes, not only for code.

First tabs

To add tabs, use the following syntax:

{% tabs group-name %}

{% tab group-name tab-name-1 %}

Content 1

{% endtab %}

{% tab group-name tab-name-2 %}

Content 2

{% endtab %}

{% endtabs %}

With this you can generate visualizations like:

  • php
  • js
  • ruby 
  • var_dump('hello');

  • console.log("hello");

  • pputs 'hello'

Another example

  • yaml
  • json 
  • hello:
  - "whatsup"
  - "hi"

  • {
  "hello": ["whatsup", "hi"]
}

Tabs for something else

  • text
  • quote
  • list

  • Regular text

  • A quote

  • Hipster list

    • brunch
    • fixie
    • raybans
    • messenger bag
entry.summary: this is what included tabs in a post could look like

entry.title: a post with typograms
entry.content:
This is an example post with some typograms code.

```typograms
+----+
|    |---> My first diagram!
+----+
```

Which generates:

+----+
|    |---> My first diagram!
+----+

Another example:

```typograms
.------------------------.
|.----------------------.|
||"https://example.com" ||
|'----------------------'|
| ______________________ |
||                      ||
||   Welcome!           ||
||                      ||
||                      ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  | username       |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  |"*******"       |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||                      ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  |   "Sign-up"    |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||                      ||
|+----------------------+|
.------------------------.
```

which generates:

.------------------------.
|.----------------------.|
||"https://example.com" ||
|'----------------------'|
| ______________________ |
||                      ||
||   Welcome!           ||
||                      ||
||                      ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  | username       |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  |"*******"       |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||                      ||
||  .----------------.  ||
||  |   "Sign-up"    |  ||
||  '----------------'  ||
||                      ||
|+----------------------+|
.------------------------.

For more examples, check out the typograms documentation.
entry.summary: this is what included typograms code could look like

entry.title: a post that can be cited

link.title: a post that can be cited

entry.content: This is an example post that can be cited. The content of the post ends here, while the citation information is automatically provided below. The only thing needed is for you to set the citation key in the front matter to true.
entry.summary: this is what a post that can be cited looks like
